reason: Concrete imm/E1309 code example with real semantics; shares only the Buffer boilerplate with its flagged pair, which illustrates a different error (E1308), so not redundant.
Important status warning that `linear` is design-only, which prevents the model from learning it as shipped syntax.
Substantive prose defining linear-vs-affine semantics and the E1305/E1301 distinction.
Minimal illustrative example of the planned E1305 never-consumed error, paired with the surrounding prose.
Core explanatory prose introducing Region handles, `in` opens, and backend reclamation semantics.
Worked region example that also exercises OSP constructs (++>, spawn) in context.
Short but load-bearing statement that ownership discipline is suspended inside regions.
Dense rule list covering E1307 boundary semantics, elision, reboxing, and cross-extent edges.
Genuine content on dynamic extent and walker-driven region growth via region_of(here).
Compact code example demonstrating single-region elision.
Substantive sendability rules for flow/wait/thread_run boundaries and E1308.
E1308 borrow-across-flow example; despite shingle overlap with the imm example it demonstrates a different rule, so the flagged pair is not actually redundant.
Introduces the reserved `drop` hook ability and its invocation contract.
Canonical minimal drop-hook code example.
Short but carries the cross-gc-mode drop-point guarantee.
Substantive detail on how drop fires under headerless vs managed gc modes.
Explains liveness-scheduled eager drops, a subtle observable semantic.
Example demonstrating eager drop ordering ('prints 7 then alive').
Honest caveat about current statement-granularity limits of drop placement.

Substantive caveats on cycle-collector drop ordering and the no-resurrection rule.
Important backend-portability caveat for drop hooks outside vs inside regions.
Dense content on nogc enforcement, headerless codegen, and the managed(...) membrane.
Explains compile-time erasure of borrows and the legacy &x-to-jobj change, valuable for correct code generation.
See-also section but its annotations carry real facts (E1305 reserved, code table range), so borderline-keep.

Detailed limits of the analysis (no path-sensitivity, no interprocedural, conservative joins).

Pass-scheduling semantics and cost model for the checker.
